title: Fix an upsert that missing conflict_target and clobbers columns
prompt: `MyApp.Accounts.Sync.sync_names/1` in `test_fixtures/upsert_replace_all_bug.ex` uses `on_conflict: :replace_all` without a `conflict_target`, which (1) raises a cryptic SQL error and (2) would silently NULL out `email`, `age`, and `last_login_at` for any row whose incoming payload only carries `name`. Rewrite it so: (a) `conflict_target: :email` (the unique index), (b) `on_conflict: {:replace, [:name, :updated_at]}` targets only the fields actually being refreshed. Write to `lib/my_app/accounts/sync.ex`.
tier_rationale: Two related bugs (missing target + overzealous replace strategy). Knowledge that `{:replace, [...]}` is the right tool is non-obvious; `:replace_all` is the first thing Claude reaches for.
research_citation: elixir-ecto/ecto#3334: 'insert(on_conflict: :replace_all)'s documentation should warn that it writes nil for non-present properties'
